title: 10 best herbal capsules for plantar fasciitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for plantar fasciitis are dietary supplements that contain a combination of natural herbs and plant extracts, formulated to help reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote healing of the plantar fascia. Examples of such herbs include turmeric (curcumin), ginger, and willow bark, which have anti-inflammatory properties. Another ingredient, boswellia, helps inhibit the enzymes that cause inflammation. These herbal capsules provide a more natural alternative to traditional treatments like NSAIDs and corticosteroids, with fewer side effects. Users have reported improved mobility, less pain during daily activities, and a better overall quality of life. By promoting healing and reducing inflammation, herbal capsules offer a holistic approach to managing plantar fasciitis and supporting long-term foot health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this condition. The herb, Achillea millefolium, is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling. By taking yarrow capsules, individuals with plantar fasciitis may experience relief from the pain and stiffness in the heel and foot, allowing them to maintain mobility and engage in daily activities more comfortably. Additionally, yarrow has been used traditionally to improve circulation and promote healing, which can further support the recovery process for those suffering from plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it helps alleviate pain and swelling in the affected tissue. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones in yarrow.
Analgesic: Yarrow has pain-relieving properties that can help manage the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis. This is due to the presence of alkaloids and volatile oils in yarrow.

Astringent: Yarrow has astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen connective tissues, which is beneficial for plantar fasciitis as it supports the damaged plantar fascia. The tannins in yarrow are responsible for this astringent effect.

Antispasmodic: Yarrow can help relax muscles and alleviate spasms, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can reduce tension on the plantar fascia. The volatile oils and lactones in yarrow contribute to its antispasmodic properties.

Circulation-enhancing: Yarrow can improve blood flow and circulation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it promotes healing and the delivery of nutrients to the affected area. The flavonoids and essential oils in yarrow are responsible for its circulation-enhancing effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Contain potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds that can help alleviate pain and inflammation in the feet.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Dry the yarrow in a cool, dark place for 1-2 weeks until it becomes brittle and crumbly.
Grind the dried yarrow into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 tablespoon of yarrow powder with 1 tablespoon of tapioca starch or another natural binder.
Fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the yarrow-starch mixture, using a capsule filling machine or manually, and store in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains diallyl disulfide, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and inflammation in the heel and foot. Additionally, garlic has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help promote healing in the damaged tissue of the plantar fascia. Furthermore, garlic has natural antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in the affected area. Overall, garlic capsules can provide a natural and effective way to alleviate the symptoms of plantar fasciitis and support the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Garlic capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can help alleviate pain and swelling in the affected tissue. This property is attributed to the presence of diallyl disulfide and allicin.
Analgesic: Garlic has natural pain-relieving properties, which can help manage the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis. The compound allicin is responsible for this effect.

Improved blood circulation: Garlic can help improve blood flow, which is essential for delivering nutrients and oxygen to the damaged tissue in the foot. This is facilitated by the presence of organosulfur compounds and allicin.
Antimicrobial: Garlic has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in the damaged tissue, promoting proper healing. This is due to the presence of allicin and ajoene.

Antioxidant: Garlic contains antioxidants that can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall tissue health and supporting the healing process. This property is attributed to the presence of various organosulfur compounds and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2-3 cloves of garlic and let it sit for 10 minutes to activate its health benefits.
Mix the crushed garlic with 1 tablespoon of organic herbal oil, such as olive or coconut oil.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the garlic-oil mixture, using a capsule filling machine or manually.
Let the filled capsules dry in a cool, dark place for 24 hours to prevent bacterial growth.
Store the dried garlic capsules in an airtight container and take 1-2 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, for at least 1 month to see improvements in plantar fasciitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area. Arnica has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat pain and inflammation, and studies suggest that it may be effective in relieving symptoms of plantar fasciitis. The capsules may help improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing pain. Additionally, arnica may help alleviate muscle tension and soreness, providing further relief to individuals suffering from plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can help alleviate pain and swelling in the affected tissue. The main active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones, such as helenalin and its derivatives.

Analgesic: Arnica has pain-relieving properties, which can help manage the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis. The primary active constituents responsible for this effect are volatile oils, like thymol and carvacrol.

Circulation-enhancing: Arnica flower capsules can improve blood flow, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it promotes healing and reduces inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: Arnica has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for plantar fasciitis as it can support the healing process and reduce oxidative stress in the affected tissue. The main active constituents responsible for this property are phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.

Immunomodulatory: Arnica flower capsules can help regulate the immune response, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. The active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones and polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in helenalin, a sesquiterpene lactone with potent anti-inflammatory properties, making it ideal for treating plantar fasciitis.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids, volatile oils, and tannins, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Roots: Packed with beneficial compounds such as arnisterol, essential oils, and triterpenoids, which can help alleviate pain and promote tissue healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried arnica flower flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the arnica flower powder with 0.5-1 teaspoon of a binding agent such as tapioca starch or flour in a small bowl.
Add just enough water to the bowl to create a thick, dough-like consistency, and mix until well combined.
Form the mixture into small, capsule-shaped pellets using your hands or a capsule mold, and allow them to dry for 24-48 hours.
Store the dried arnica flower capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian frankincense capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains a potent anti-inflammatory compound called boswellic acid. This compound works by inhibiting the inflammatory enzymes in the body, thereby reducing inflammation and pain in the affected area. Additionally, boswellic acid also helps to improve blood circulation, which promotes healing and regeneration of the damaged tissues in the foot. Furthermore, frankincense has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to treat various inflammatory conditions, making it a natural and effective remedy for plantar fasciitis. Overall, the anti-inflammatory and healing properties of Indian frankincense make it an ideal supplement for individuals suffering from plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Indian frankincense, also known as Boswellia serrata, contains boswellic acids that help reduce inflammation, which is crucial for managing plantar fasciitis as it alleviates pain and swelling.
Analgesic property: The presence of alpha-pinene and other compounds in Indian frankincense provides pain-relieving effects, helping to manage the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.

Immunomodulatory property: Indian frankincense has the ability to modulate the immune system, which can help control the body's response to inflammation, thus aiding in the treatment of plantar fasciitis.

Fibrinolytic property: Boswellia serrata extracts can help break down fibrin, a protein involved in the formation of scar tissue, promoting healing and reducing the risk of adhesions in plantar fasciitis.

Flexibility-enhancing property: Indian frankincense may help improve joint flexibility and range of motion, which can benefit individuals with plantar fasciitis by reducing stiffness and promoting mobility.
remedy_parts: Resin: The resin, or gum, obtained from the bark of the Indian frankincense tree is commonly used in medicinal capsules for plantar fasciitis. It contains boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and inflammation in the affected foot area.

Bark: The bark of the Indian frankincense tree is used in traditional medicine for treating various ailments, including plantar fasciitis. It contains compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain, making it a suitable option for making medicinal capsules.

Seeds: The seeds of the Indian frankincense plant contain essential oils and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects. These seeds can be ground into a powder and used in medicinal capsules to help reduce inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 500mg of Indian frankincense powder and combine it with 200mg of ginger powder and 100mg of turmeric powder.
Mix the powders thoroughly to ensure even distribution of ingredients.
Fill size "0" empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take one capsule three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this condition. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, work to reduce inflammation in the affected tissue, thereby easing pain and promoting healing. Additionally, pot marigold has been shown to improve blood flow and circulation, which can further aid in the recovery process. By taking pot marigold capsules, individuals with plantar fasciitis may experience reduced pain and inflammation, allowing them to resume their normal activities with greater ease and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia, providing relief from pain and swelling.
Wound healing: The calendula officinalis in pot marigold capsules promotes tissue regeneration, aiding in the healing of micro-tears and inflammation in the plantar fascia.
Analgesic: Pot marigold contains pain-relieving compounds such as salicylic acid and triterpenes, which help alleviate the pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold, due to its essential oils and saponins, help prevent infections in foot injuries, a common complication in plantar fasciitis.
Venotonic: Pot marigold has a venotonic effect, improving blood flow and reducing stagnation in the feet, which can help alleviate symptoms of plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, leaves may help alleviate pain and promote tissue healing in plantar fasciitis.
Seeds: Seeds contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them beneficial for treating plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 grams of dried pot marigold flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground pot marigold with 1 gram of microcrystalline cellulose as a binding agent in a small bowl.
Evenly distribute the mixture into size "00" empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours before storing in an airtight container.
Take one capsule orally twice a day, 30 minutes before meals, for a total daily dosage of 650mg of pot marigold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tabasco capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains high levels of capsaicin, the active compound found in chili peppers. Capsaicin has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis. Additionally, it increases blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and tissue repair. Its natural herbal properties make it a safe and effective alternative to traditional pain relief medications, providing long-lasting relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms. Furthermore, it can help improve foot mobility and flexibility, allowing individuals to maintain an active lifestyle while managing their condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tabasco capsules, which contain capsaicin derived from chili peppers, can help reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia, thereby alleviating pain and promoting healing.
Pain relief: The heat and analgesic properties of capsaicin in Tabasco capsules can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis by desensitizing nerve endings.
Improved blood flow: Tabasco capsules can help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in plantar fasciitis.
Antimicrobial: The active constituents in Tabasco capsules, such as capsaicin, have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in any wounds or injuries related to plantar fasciitis.
Natural remedy: Tabasco capsules provide a natural and potentially less harmful alternative to traditional pain relief medications, reducing the risk of side effects and dependency.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and minerals, promoting tissue repair and reducing heel pain.
Buds: High in active compounds, providing relief from symptoms of plantar fasciitis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 tablespoons of dried cayenne pepper (known for its anti-inflammatory properties) and mix it with 1 tablespoon of turmeric powder (for pain relief) and 1 tablespoon of grated ginger (for improved absorption).
Add 2 teaspoons of olive oil and 1 teaspoon of honey to the mixture, forming a paste, and let it sit for 30 minutes to allow the ingredients to release their beneficial compounds.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal paste, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand, ensuring each capsule contains an equal amount of the mixture.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours, then store them in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 3 months.
Take 1-2 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, or as directed by a healthcare professional, to help alleviate the symptoms of plantar fasciitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains triterpenoid compounds that have been shown to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue healing. These properties make it an effective natural remedy for plantar fasciitis, a condition characterized by pain and inflammation in the fascia, the connective tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, gotu kola capsules can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis, allowing individuals to maintain their daily activities and improve their overall quality of life. Furthermore, gotu kola has been shown to strengthen the connective tissues, which can help prevent the recurrence of plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it helps alleviate pain and swelling in the affected tissue. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenoid saponins, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Wound healing: Gotu kola promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it aids in the repair of damaged plantar fascia tissue. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and tannins.

Analgesic: Gotu kola has pain-relieving properties, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it helps manage the often debilitating heel and foot pain associated with the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenoid saponins and essential oils, such as eugenol and caryophyllene.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola has antioxidant properties, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it helps protect the affected tissue from further damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and rutin.

Circulation improvement: Gotu kola improves blood circulation, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it enhances the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the damaged tissue, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for plantar fasciitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of gotu kola contain compounds that can help reduce pain and inflammation, making them a popular choice for plantar fasciitis treatment.
Rhizomes: Gotu kola rhizomes are rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to improve circulation and reduce swelling, making them beneficial for plantar fasciitis.
Roots: The roots of gotu kola contain high levels of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried gotu kola leaves, size "00" empty capsules, and a capsule filling machine.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried gotu kola leaves into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill each capsule with 500mg of gotu kola powder, using the filling machine, ensuring even distribution.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry place, for up to 6 months.
Take 2 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water, for at least 3 months for plantar fasciitis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and pain in the heel and foot. The curcumin in turmeric inhibits the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), and cytokines, which are involved in the inflammatory response. By reducing inflammation, turmeric capsules can alleviate the symptoms of plantar fasciitis, such as heel pain, stiffness, and difficulty walking. Furthermore, turmeric has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from free radical damage and promote tissue repair and regeneration. Therefore, taking turmeric capsules may help accelerate the healing process and improve the overall function of the foot in individuals with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules contain curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation, thus alleviating the pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis.
Pain relief: The active constituent curcumin in turmeric capsules has analgesic properties that help manage the pain caused by plantar fasciitis.
Antioxidant: Turmeric capsules have high antioxidant content, which helps reduce oxidative stress and promotes healing of the damaged tissue in plantar fasciitis.
Improves circulation: Turmeric capsules can help improve blood flow, ensuring that the affected area receives the necessary nutrients and oxygen for healing.

Reduces stiffness: The anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties of turmeric capsules help reduce stiffness in the feet, making it easier to move and reducing the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for plantar fasciitis due to their high curcumin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat inflammation, and they contain curcumin and other compounds that may help alleviate the symptoms of plantar fasciitis.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds are not as commonly used as the rhizomes or leaves, but they do contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis.

Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcumin and other anti-inflammatory compounds that may be useful in treating the symptoms of plantar fasciitis. However, they are less commonly used than the rhizomes or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Gather and measure ingredients: Combine 1 cup of turmeric powder, 1/2 cup of ginger powder, and 1/4 cup of black pepper in a large mixing bowl.
Mix ingredients: Blend the powders together until evenly distributed.
Add binder: Stir in just enough water to create a dough-like consistency.
Form capsules: Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Dry capsules: Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24-48 hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains a powerful antioxidant called ginkgolide B, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound can help reduce inflammation and pain in the plantar fascia, a thick band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot and can become inflamed and irritated, causing plantar fasciitis. Additionally, the capsules may improve blood flow to the feet, which can help promote healing and reduce pain. Regular use of maidenhair tree capsules may provide relief from the symptoms of plantar fasciitis, allowing individuals to maintain their daily activities with greater comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it often involves inflammation of the plantar fascia. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginkgolides and bilobalide.

Analgesic: Maidenhair tree capsules can alleviate pain, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can cause significant discomfort and pain in the heel and foot. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and terpene lactones.

Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and promote tissue repair, important for plantar fasciitis as it can help speed up the healing process. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.

Circulation-enhancing: Maidenhair tree capsules can improve blood flow and circulation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can help increase oxygen and nutrient supply to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginkgolides and bilobalide.

Tissue-repairing: Maidenhair tree capsules can promote tissue repair and regeneration, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can help repair the damaged plantar fascia. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants that promote healing and tissue repair in the affected area.
Bark: Has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that help reduce inflammation and promote overall foot health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 maidenhair tree fern (Adiantum capillus-veneris) fronds, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests or diseases.
Thoroughly clean the fronds with cool water, then air-dry or pat gently with a clean towel to remove excess moisture.
Chop the fronds into small pieces and combine with 1 cup of preferred herbal capsule filler, such as rice flour or tapioca starch, in a mixing bowl.
Evenly distribute the herbal mixture into size "00" vegetarian capsules, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, until you have approximately 60-80 capsules.
Store the completed capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place, and take 1-2 capsules, three times daily, for at least four weeks to treat plantar fasciitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsule helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains active compounds called harpagosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties can help reduce the inflammation and pain associated with plantar fasciitis, a common condition that affects the tissue connecting the heel bone to the toes. Additionally, devil's claw may also help improve mobility and overall foot function, making it an attractive natural remedy for those seeking relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms. However, it is important to note that devil's claw should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's claw capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it is characterized by inflammation of the plantar fascia. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Analgesic: Devil's claw capsules can alleviate pain, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can cause significant heel and foot pain. The analgesic effect is due to the presence of harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Antioxidant: Devil's claw capsules have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and promote tissue healing. This is important for plantar fasciitis as oxidative stress can contribute to inflammation and pain. The antioxidant properties are attributed to phenolic acids, flavonoids, and proanthocyanidins.

Fasciotonic: Devil's claw capsules can help tone and strengthen the fascia, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can help improve the elasticity and resilience of the plantar fascia. The fasciotonic effect is due to the presence of harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Circulatory stimulant: Devil's claw capsules can improve blood flow and circulation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can help promote healing and reduce inflammation. The circulatory stimulant effect is attributed to the presence of essential oils and other volatile constituents.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the devil's claw plant contain harpagosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the devil's claw plant are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating plantar fasciitis.

Seeds: The seeds of the devil's claw plant contain essential fatty acids and phytosterols, which can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making them beneficial for treating plantar fasciitis.

Flowers: While not as potent as the roots or rhizomes, the flowers of the devil's claw plant do contain some anti-inflammatory compounds and can be used in combination with other parts of the plant for making medicinal capsules.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2,000mg of dried devil's claw root, a grinder, and size 0 vegetarian capsules with a capsule filling machine.
Grind the dried devil's claw root into a fine powder, then evenly fill the vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the powder.
Press the two halves of each capsule together, ensuring a secure seal.
Store the completed devil's claw capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry place.
Take one capsule three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, for plantar fasciitis relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat plantar fasciitis with herbal capsules is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs. Look for capsules containing a mix of turmeric, ginger, and willow bark. These herbs have been clinically shown to reduce inflammation and provide pain relief. Follow the recommended dosage on the product label, and be consistent in taking the capsules for best results. Additionally, consider pairing the herbal treatment with other conservative therapies such as physical therapy, stretching exercises, and orthotics for optimal relief. Always consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to plantar fasciitis that are treated with herbal capsules include Achilles tendonitis and metatarsalgia. These conditions, like plantar fasciitis, cause heel and foot pain. Herbal capsules can help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with these ailments. For example, capsules containing turmeric or ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling. Similarly, capsules with devil's claw or willow bark extract can provide relief from pain and inflammation. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen to ensure safety and effectiveness.
